sub: Judgement, confession, no hedging. Someone's getting read.

1.name: Opening Shots
q: On three, both say how tonight is actually going out of ten.~Different numbers? The lower one explains. No diplomatic 7s.
Guess what I thought about you in the first ten seconds. I'll tell you how close you got.
Say the least flattering true thing you've noticed about me so far.~It has to be true. That's the only rule.
What's an opinion you hold that would make most people here think less of you?
Five seconds, first instinct: what am I bad at? Go.
Point at whichever of us is more likely to ruin this. Three, two, one — point.~Now defend it.
What's something everyone agrees is good that you genuinely think is overrated?
Which of us needs this more? Answer honestly, not politely.
Rate my confidence out of ten. Then tell me whether that was a compliment.
What did you assume about me that you've already had to correct?
Say the text you'd send your friend about me tonight. Word for word, out loud.
What's a thing you judge people for instantly and refuse to feel bad about?
Am I your type?~'Sort of' isn't an answer. Pick a side.
Who's trying harder right now — you or me? On three, both point.
What's the most annoying thing I've done since we sat down? Something small counts.
What's a compliment people give you that you know is a lie?
Say something you believe that you've learned to stop saying out loud.
Pick one and commit: I'm someone you'll remember, or someone you'll forget by Thursday.
What's the pettiest reason you've ever written someone off completely?
What's a red flag you've already clocked in me? There's at least one.
Which one of us is more full of it? Both answer at the same time.
What do you actually want from tonight? The real answer, not the polite one.
What's the last lie you told, and who got it?
Say the most arrogant true thing about yourself.~No hedging, no laughing it off at the end.
If you had to sabotage this in one sentence, what would you say?
What's something you find attractive that you're a bit embarrassed by?
What have you already decided about me that you haven't said?
What's a widely-loved thing you'd happily see wiped off the earth?
How much of what you've told me tonight has been performance? Out of ten.
What's the question you're hoping I don't ask?

2.name: No Take-Backs
q: What's the worst thing you've ever thought about someone you love?~You don't have to say who.
What have you done that you'd be ashamed for me to know?~Don't tell me what. Just tell me how bad, out of ten.
You can skip this card. But if you do, I get one question and you answer it properly.
Who have you been unfair to and never made right?
What's the most selfish thing you've ever done that worked out perfectly for you?
Argue for something you actually find indefensible. Thirty seconds. Make it good.
What's the meanest thing you've ever said — and did you mean it?
Who do you secretly think you're better than?
What's a rule you break constantly and have no intention of stopping?
Guess my worst quality.~Right, and I owe you a real confession. Wrong, and you owe me one.
What's something you want that you'd be judged for admitting?
What's the last thing you did purely because it would look good to other people?
Tell me about a time you were the villain.~Not the misunderstood version. The actual villain.
What do you lie about most often — and why that thing specifically?
What's something true about you that would make me walk?
What's the most manipulative thing you've done and got away with?
What's a compliment you fish for constantly? Be specific about how you do it.
What are you competing with your friends about that you'd never admit to?
What are you most defensive about?~Now let me poke it.
Which of your good qualities is mostly an act at this point?
What have you never apologised for and never will?
What would the person who likes you least say about you? Say it in their voice.
What's a thought you have regularly that you'd never say out loud?
What's the closest you've come to genuinely wrecking your own life?
Who did you lose that was your fault?
What do you actually think about when you can't sleep?
What's the ugliest true thing about why you're here tonight?
What's a thing you've forgiven yourself for that you probably shouldn't have?
Who in your life would be quietly relieved if you vanished for a year?
What's the worst advice you've given that someone actually took?

3.name: Too Far
q: What have you been thinking for the last ten minutes and not saying?~Say it exactly. Not the cleaned-up version.
On three, both say whether you want this to go anywhere.~Whatever comes out, you both live with it.
What's the thing about me you'd change if you could?
Say the boldest thing you could say to me right now. It has to have actual risk in it.
What am I doing tonight that's working on you?~Name the exact moment.
Have you been performing tonight? Which parts?
What would you regret not saying to me before this ends?~So say it now instead.
Guess what I'm hoping happens next. I'll tell you if you're right.
What's the question you've wanted to ask me all night?~Ask it. Out loud. Now.
Where does this actually go? Both answer at once — no hedging, no 'we'll see'.
Say the thing you'd only say if you knew you'd never see me again.
What did you decide about me in the first minute that you still think is true?
Am I what you were hoping for?~The honest answer costs something. Pay it.
What are you scared this turns into?
The truth about how much you like me. A number's allowed — but only if you explain it.
What do you want to do right now that you're not going to do?
What would you tell me if you were braver?
What's the story you'll tell about tonight?~Tell it now, to my face.
What have I got wrong about you tonight that you let slide?
If this is it — if we never do this again — what do you want me to know?
Say the thing you're most afraid I'll agree with.
What do you want from me that you haven't asked for?
Which of us is more into this? Both point on three.~Then talk about the answer.
What's the least honest thing you've said to me tonight?~Correct it now.
What's the most honest thing you can say about how you feel right this second?

wild: Hand over your phone. They read your last three sent messages out loud. No deleting first.
Swap phones. Sixty seconds, look wherever you want. Then they get the same.
They get to ask you one question — anything — and you answer it properly. Now.
Say the meanest true thing about me, then the kindest. In that order, no gap in between.
Hold eye contact until one of you says something real. Not until someone laughs — until someone says something real.
They pick something you're wearing or carrying. You give them the actual story, not the short version.
Do an impression of me. Be unkind about it. I'm not allowed to react.
Read out your last three searches. No scrolling past anything.
Say something you think will make them like you less. Then find out whether it did.
They rate you out of ten, out loud, and have to justify it.~You may not respond for thirty seconds.
Trade your worst secret of the night. They go first — you have to match it or beat it.
Whisper the thing you'd never say at normal volume. Then say it again, properly.
Both write down what you think happens after tonight. Show each other at the same time.
Say the last thing you'd want the other one to know about you. Out loud, no build-up.
final: Last card. Whatever you've been managing all night — the thing you kept just under the surface — stop managing it.
Say it plainly, once. Don't decorate it, and don't put a joke on the end.
Now you both know. Do something about it or don't — but you don't get to pretend it wasn't said.
